tipRules.tip: Johnston: On Lake Ontario and the 1000 Islands, a drop-shot is the go-to for targeting smallmouth on the open lake.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: Rod power matters when flipping. I switched from a 4-power GLX flipping stick to a 5-power NRX and the stiffer butt section was so powerful I was ripping holes in fish mouths on the hookset, losing four 4-pounders. Going down to a 4-power NRX solved it - never lost a fish on the jig afterward.

Johnston: On the Potomac, I found fish out in the grass, not in the real thick stuff. I was casting to catch them, not flipping. When that failed due to muddy water from rain, I moved to flipping grass on the Greenway Flats.
Johnston: We're not really guys that do a lot of research on the Internet. We just back the boat in and figure it out.
